english:
[pagebreak]

Discovering Ruunvald Vol. I by Moric Sidrey

[pagebreak]

I have decided to document our expedition to find Ruunvald in my journals, with hopes that, should we fail, it will bring illumination to those who follow us. I myself have stood on the shoulders of academic giants to get where we are today, spending endless hours in libraries and private collections. I am certain that there is some artifact of great power to be found in those ruins, one that the Vigilants could find useful in their mission. I do not mean to sound pompous, but I feel as if I am on the precipice of my destiny with this quest. I am certain we are digging in the right place. I can feel it in my bones, and I dream of finding Ruunvald at night. Even in my waking hours, I can almost hear a reassuring voice telling me we are going the right way. The expedition has had a great deal of luck so far. After only a few weeks, the first tunnel broke through into a large shaft, leading downward towards where I suspect to find Ruunvald. With just a few bits of carpentry, we've established our first base camp within the mountains. If we continue to be this fortuitous, we shall reach our goal in record time!

M. Sidrey
chinese:
[pagebreak]

探索鲁恩瓦德 第一卷 墨里克·西德利 著

[pagebreak]

我决定在日记上记录我们寻找鲁恩瓦德的探险，如果我们失败了，希望它能给后继者带来点启发。我自己也是站在学术巨擘们的肩膀上，在图书馆和私人收藏馆中花费了数不尽的时光，才有如今的成就的。我相信，在这遗迹中能找到某种拥有巨大力量的东西，在警戒者的任务中派上用场。 我不想说的太自负，不过我觉得这次任务中我的命运似乎处在转折点上。我确信我们挖对了地方。我在心底里能感受到，我夜里做梦都梦见找到鲁恩瓦德。即使在我醒着的时候，我也几乎能听到一个令人安心的声音，跟我说我们走对路了。 到目前为止，探险队运气不错。仅仅几周之后，第一条隧道就打通了，形成了一座巨大的竖井，向下通往我觉得能找到鲁恩瓦德的地方。我们做了一点点木匠活，就在山里搭建起了第一座营地。如果我们还能这么幸运的话，我们达成目标的时间会创纪录的！

M.西德利

english:
[pagebreak]

Journal of Mirtil Angoth Translated from Falmer Text by Calcelmo of Markarth

[pagebreak]

4th of Evening Star I used to dream of fighting in battles like my Father. He had begun teaching me to fight the moment I was able to pick up a blade. Mother had argued that I was too young, but he paid her no mind. I can still remember the elation I felt the first time I bested Father in a match and the look of pride on his face. If it were up to him I know he would have allowed me to join him in battle. With me at his side he may have fared better. Now with Father and so many others slain, the Old Ones claim we are left with too few warriors to continue the fight. I was not the only Young One to speak out in protest, but our small voices went unheard. It has been decided that we must flee to seek help and protection. [pagebreak] 8th of Evening Star News has reached us that the great Snow Prince has fallen in battle. The urgency to go into hiding has left many of us scattered and those of us still together unsure of which direction to turn. In the long hours of night we keep huddled together always fearing the worst until the first light of the blessed sun. May Auri-El guide our footsteps. [pagebreak] 13th of Evening Star In the night I overheard the Old Ones whispering secrets of the underground and the Dwemer who dwell there. I thought back on stories Father once told me of these dwarves, heroic tales of honor and glory. The Old Ones must know of these stories for it has been decided that we will change course upon first light. I feel hopeful that the Dwemer will help us to avenge our fallen and reclaim our land.
chinese:
[pagebreak]

米尔蒂尔·安格斯的日记 译自伐莫文献 马卡斯城的卡塞莫 译

[pagebreak]

夜星月4日 我曾梦想像我父亲一样战斗。当我能举得起剑的时候，他就开始教我战斗了。母亲吵着说我太小了，不过他没有理会。我还记得第一次在比试中胜过我父亲的喜悦以及他脸上的骄傲。如果由他决定的话，我知道他会让我加入战斗的。有我在他身旁，他可能会表现得更好。现在我父亲还有其他许多人都被杀了，长老们说我们剩下的战士太少，没法继续战斗了。我不是唯一一个大声抗议的青年，然而我们微弱的声音却没人理会。已经决定了，我们必须逃走，去寻求帮助和庇护。 [pagebreak] 夜星月8日 伟大的雪王子在战斗中牺牲的消息传到了我们这里。紧迫的躲避行动使我们许多人走散了，而我们这些仍然在一起的人不知道该往哪个方向走。在漫漫长夜，我们总是挤成一团，担心最坏的事情发生，直到天亮。愿奥瑞-艾尔指引我们前进。 [pagebreak] 夜星月13日 夜里，我无意间听见长老们小声说着关于地下以及居住在那里的锻莫的秘密。我想起父亲曾经给我讲过关于锻莫的故事，关于荣耀与荣誉的英雄传说。长老们一定知道这些故事，因为他们决定我们天一亮就改变方向。希望锻莫能帮我们复仇，收复我们的土地。

[pagebreak]

夜星月4日 我曾梦想像我父亲一样战斗。当我能举得起剑的时候，他就开始教我战斗了。母亲吵着说我太小了，不过他没有理会。我还记得第一次在比试中胜过我父亲的喜悦以及他脸上的骄傲。如果由他决定的话，我知道他会让我加入战斗的。有我在他身旁，他可能会表现得更好。现在我父亲还有其他许多人都被杀了，长老们说我们剩下的战士太少，没法继续战斗了。我不是唯一一个大声抗议的青年，然而我们微弱的声音却没人理会。已经决定了，我们必须逃走，去寻求帮助和庇护。 [pagebreak] 夜星月8日 伟大的雪王子在战斗中牺牲的消息传到了我们这里。紧迫的躲避行动使我们许多人走散了，而我们这些仍然在一起的人不知道该往哪个方向走。在漫漫长夜，我们总是挤成一团，担心最坏的事情发生，直到天亮。愿奥瑞-艾尔指引我们前进。 [pagebreak] 夜星月13日 夜里，我无意间听见长老们小声说着关于地下以及居住在那里的锻莫的秘密。我想起父亲曾经给我讲过关于锻莫的故事，关于荣耀与荣誉的英雄传说。长老们一定知道这些故事，因为他们决定我们天一亮就改变方向。希望锻莫能帮我们复仇，收复我们的土地。

english:
[pagebreak]

The Scripture of Minorne by Moric Sidrey

[pagebreak]

Glory be to Minorne! Glory be to the mistress of all! My life for you, oh beautiful savior! Where once my feeble ramblings seemed so grand, I now realize they are but scratches on parchment, unworthy of you. Oh, that I could properly describe you, I would write a thousand testaments to you! Damn my tiny thoughts, if only I were wiser! Minorne asks that we bring more here for her, more to worship her and do her bidding! I have sent word to the Hall of the Vigilants to come. A simple lie was told, for they would not understand. Not until they saw her, o glorious Minorne! But she is fearful! There are fools in this world that do not heed to her beautiful voice. The guard, Florentius, sent from the Beacon, he still prays to Arkay, an absentee god who pales in comparison to Minorne! I will pray to the goddess I can see! May he rot in his cage! Oh, sweet, sweet, Minorne...
chinese:
[pagebreak]

蜜诺妮的圣典 墨里克·西德利 著

[pagebreak]

荣耀属于蜜诺妮！荣耀属于万物的女主人！美丽的救世主啊，我将生命献给您！曾经我那无力的胡言乱语是如此苍白，如今我明白它们不过是羊皮纸上的划痕，配不上您！啊，若我能正确地将您描绘出来，我将为您写下千言万语。我的思想太渺小了，我若能再智慧些就好了！ 蜜诺妮让我给她带更多的人，让更多人崇拜她，服从她！我已经给警戒者大厅捎了信让他们过来。一句简简单单的谎言，因为他们在见到她之前是不会理解的，美丽的蜜诺妮啊！ 然而她很害怕！这世上有很多傻瓜不听从她那优美的声音。从烽火台派来的守卫——佛伦提乌斯——仍在向阿尔凯祈祷，与蜜诺妮相比这位看不见的神苍白无比。我要向我所见的女神祈祷。愿佛伦提乌斯烂在笼子里！ 啊，最亲爱的蜜诺妮……
englishNormalized:
[pagebreak]

the scripture of minorne by moric sidrey

[pagebreak]

glory be to minorne! glory be to the mistress of all! my life for you, oh beautiful savior! where once my feeble ramblings seemed so grand, i now realize they are but scratches on parchment, unworthy of you. oh, that i could properly describe you, i would write a thousand testaments to you! damn my tiny thoughts, if only i were wiser! minorne asks that we bring more here for her, more to worship her and do her bidding! i have sent word to the hall of the vigilants to come. a simple lie was told, for they would not understand. not until they saw her, o glorious minorne! but she is fearful! there are fools in this world that do not heed to her beautiful voice. the guard, florentius, sent from the beacon, he still prays to arkay, an absentee god who pales in comparison to minorne! i will pray to the goddess i can see! may he rot in his cage! oh, sweet, sweet, minorne...
chineseNormalized:
[pagebreak]

蜜诺妮的圣典 墨里克·西德利 著

[pagebreak]

荣耀属于蜜诺妮！荣耀属于万物的女主人！美丽的救世主啊，我将生命献给您！曾经我那无力的胡言乱语是如此苍白，如今我明白它们不过是羊皮纸上的划痕，配不上您！啊，若我能正确地将您描绘出来，我将为您写下千言万语。我的思想太渺小了，我若能再智慧些就好了！ 蜜诺妮让我给她带更多的人，让更多人崇拜她，服从她！我已经给警戒者大厅捎了信让他们过来。一句简简单单的谎言，因为他们在见到她之前是不会理解的，美丽的蜜诺妮啊！ 然而她很害怕！这世上有很多傻瓜不听从她那优美的声音。从烽火台派来的守卫——佛伦提乌斯——仍在向阿尔凯祈祷，与蜜诺妮相比这位看不见的神苍白无比。我要向我所见的女神祈祷。愿佛伦提乌斯烂在笼子里！ 啊，最亲爱的蜜诺妮……
agentText:
Addon: Dawnguard EDID: DLC1RuunvaldJournal06 Record: BOOK:DESC SID: 001F08 English: [pagebreak]

The Scripture of Minorne by Moric Sidrey

[pagebreak]

Glory be to Minorne! Glory be to the mistress of all! My life for you, oh beautiful savior! Where once my feeble ramblings seemed so grand, I now realize they are but scratches on parchment, unworthy of you. Oh, that I could properly describe you, I would write a thousand testaments to you! Damn my tiny thoughts, if only I were wiser! Minorne asks that we bring more here for her, more to worship her and do her bidding! I have sent word to the Hall of the Vigilants to come. A simple lie was told, for they would not understand. Not until they saw her, o glorious Minorne! But she is fearful! There are fools in this world that do not heed to her beautiful voice. The guard, Florentius, sent from the Beacon, he still prays to Arkay, an absentee god who pales in comparison to Minorne! I will pray to the goddess I can see! May he rot in his cage! Oh, sweet, sweet, Minorne... Chinese: [pagebreak]

english:
[pagebreak]

TOUCHING THE SKY PARMION SALDOR

[pagebreak]

MANY OF THE MOST DEDICATED SNOW ELVES ONCE COMMITTED THEMSELVES TO A TIRELESS JOURNEY THROUGH THE CHANTRY TO THE TEMPLE THEY CARRIED WITH THEM THE PARAMOUNT DESIRE TO BECOME ONE WITH THEIR GOD AURIEL THOUGH ALL SET OUT WITH THE DETERMINATION TO PROVE THEIR WORTH, FEW WERE PREPARED FOR THE TRIALS THAT LAY AHEAD FOR THE PATH TO AURIEL WAS NOT WITHOUT ITS TRIBULATIONS THE PILGRIMS STRUGGLED NOT ONLY AGAINST THE NATURAL ELEMENTS OF THE TREACHEROUS VALE BUT WITH A MYRIAD OF TESTS UPON THEIR FAITH AND LOYALTY IT IS TOLD THAT MANY SIMPLY COULD NOT CONTINUE ON THE PATH FOR LONG BEFORE TURNING BACK SOME ARGUE THAT THE REBUKE THEY ENDURED UPON THEIR RETURN WAS CRUELER THAN ANY PUNISHMENT THAT MAY HAVE LAY AHEAD OF THEM ALONG THE PATH. IN THEIR FAILED ATTEMPT THEY WERE FORCED TO LIVE IN THE SHADOW OF THOSE WHO DID CONTINUE ON TO ACHIEVE THE GREAT GLORY AND HONOR OF ASCENSION INTO THE LIGHT FOREVER AFTER THEIR FAITH AND LOYALTY IN AURIEL WOULD BE SCRUTINIZED AND THEIR REMAINING DAYS FILLED WITH SHAME AND REGRET TALES OF THOSE WHO REACHED THE INNER SANCTUM ARE NOT WITHOUT THEIR SHARE OF WOES. IT IS SAID UPON THEIR ARRIVAL THAT MANY WERE MERE SHELLS OF THE PERSON THEY HAD ONCE BEEN SOME WERE QUITE MAD FROM SLEEPLESSNESS AND STARVED TO THE POINT OF FRAILTY BY THE END OF THEIR JOURNEY THE MARVEL THAT THEY HAD STRENGTH YET TO CARRY THEIR VESSEL AND ASCEND THE STAIRS OF THE TEMPLE WAS THE LAST TRUE TESTAMENT OF THEIR LOYALTY REGARDLESS OF EACH INDIVIDUALS TALE THE FINAL WORDS REMAIN EERILY SIMILAR IT IS SAID THAT EVERY PILGRIM ASCENDED BATHED IN LIGHT A LOOK OF RELIEF AND CONTENTMENT ON THEIR FACE

englishNormalized:
[pagebreak]

touching the sky parmion saldor

[pagebreak]

many of the most dedicated snow elves once committed themselves to a tireless journey through the chantry to the temple they carried with them the paramount desire to become one with their god auriel though all set out with the determination to prove their worth, few were prepared for the trials that lay ahead for the path to auriel was not without its tribulations the pilgrims struggled not only against the natural elements of the treacherous vale but with a myriad of tests upon their faith and loyalty it is told that many simply could not continue on the path for long before turning back some argue that the rebuke they endured upon their return was crueler than any punishment that may have lay ahead of them along the path. in their failed attempt they were forced to live in the shadow of those who did continue on to achieve the great glory and honor of ascension into the light forever after their faith and loyalty in auriel would be scrutinized and their remaining days filled with shame and regret tales of those who reached the inner sanctum are not without their share of woes. it is said upon their arrival that many were mere shells of the person they had once been some were quite mad from sleeplessness and starved to the point of frailty by the end of their journey the marvel that they had strength yet to carry their vessel and ascend the stairs of the temple was the last true testament of their loyalty regardless of each individuals tale the final words remain eerily similar it is said that every pilgrim ascended bathed in light a look of relief and contentment on their face

english:
[pagebreak]

Touching the Sky by Parmion Saldor

Translated from Falmer Text by Calcelmo of Markarth [pagebreak]

any of the most dedicated snow elves once committed themselves to a tireless journey through the Chantry to the Inner Sanctum. They carried with them the paramount desire to become one with their god, Auri-El. Though all set out with the determination to prove their worth, few were prepared for the trials that lay ahead. For the path to Auri-El was not without its tribulations. The pilgrims struggled not only against the natural elements of the treacherous vale, but with a myriad of tests upon their faith and loyalty. It is told that many simply could not continue on the path for long before turning back. Some argue that the rebuke they endured upon their return was crueler than any punishment that may have lay ahead of them along the path. In their failed attempt, they were forced to live in the shadow of those who did continue on to achieve the great glory and honor of ascension into the light. Forever after, their faith and loyalty in Auri-El would be scrutinized and their remaining days filled with shame and regret. Tales of those who reached the Inner Sanctum are not without their share of woes. It is said upon their arrival that many were mere shells of the person they had once been. Some were quite mad from sleeplessness and starved to the point of frailty. By the end of their journey, the marvel that they had strength yet to carry their vessel and ascend the stairs of the temple was the last true testament of their loyalty. Regardless of each individual's tale, the final words remain eerily similar. It is said that every pilgrim ascended, bathed in light, a look of relief and contentment on their face.
chinese:
[pagebreak]

触及天空 帕米昂·萨多 著 译自伐莫文献 马卡斯城的卡塞莫 译

[pagebreak]

许多虔诚至极的雪精灵曾将自己献身于从神殿到内殿那不知疲倦的旅程。他们怀着与他们的神——奥瑞-艾尔——融为一体的崇高愿望。尽管所有人都下决心要证明自己的价值，但很少有人为前方的考验做好准备。因为通向奥瑞-艾尔的道路并非一帆风顺。朝圣者们不仅要与险恶山谷的自然环境做斗争，还要经历无数对他们的信仰与虔诚的考验。 据传，很多人在这条路上根本走不了多远就折返了。一些人认为他们返回后所遭受的谴责要比前方道路上可能面临的任何惩罚都要残酷。由于他们的尝试失败了，他们要被迫生活在继续前进，取得了升入光芒的伟大荣光的那些人的阴影中。从此之后，他们对于奥瑞-艾尔的信仰与虔诚会受到审查，而他们的余生会充满愧疚与悔恨。 那些抵达内殿者的故事也不是一点苦难都没有。据说当他们抵达时，许多人已经是一具行尸走肉了。有些人因失眠而发狂，而有些人饿到了虚脱。在他们旅途的最后，仍有力气举着水壶爬上圣殿的台阶，就是对其虔诚的最终考验。不管每个人的故事有什么不同，结尾都非常相似。据说，每一位朝圣者都拾级而上，沐浴在阳光中，面露欣慰与满足之情。

english:
[pagebreak]

Diary of Faire Agarwen Translated from Falmer Text by Calcelmo of Markarth [pagebreak]

Forward The dates noted in this diary are translated literally. This verbiage matches no known modern measure of time, and is assumed to be a custom form of counting the days and months. Excavations of ancient Falmer slave quarters have turned up brass vessels, very similar to a deep bowl, with twenty markings crudely etched onto the inside. Falmer scholars theorize that this bowl would be placed under a drip of water coming from an overhanging rock and as the bowl filled, the water's level would reach these markings, thus indicating a crude passage of time. Because of this diary, the vessel has been called a "kulniir," a primitive Falmer timekeeping device. [pagebreak]

Third Marking, Tenth Kulniir It feels like years since we were forced into hiding. I dare not write where we stay for fear of endangering the good people of this house should this diary be discovered. We have been shown a kindness by this family once known to the Snow Prince. Even in death his great influence has ensured our safety. We were separated from many of our kin along the road when it became increasingly difficult to travel discreetly in our numbers. We were forced to go our separate ways and travel only at night. I have heard no news of where the others may have gone and fear I never shall. Our lives are forever changed. [pagebreak] Seventh Marking, Tenth Kulniir In the night I find it difficult not to focus on times past. There are moments in my rest when I still hear the laughter of Young Ones at play in the valley. Other times I see the pale flicker of happy moments which were once so common in the land of the Snow Elves. I try not to dwell on these memories too long. Often our surroundings make it impossible to dwell on any happiness. We have been locked together in such close quarters for so long we grow tired of each other's company. Even the strongest of us have faltered with nothing to do but think on what is lost. I wake each day to forlorn faces and am reminded of where we are and all we have left behind. We are all yearning for a day when we can emerge from hiding and walk freely in the light once more. But I fear we are losing all hope that such a day will ever come. [pagebreak] Tenth Marking, Tenth Kulniir I tire of the tears of women and children. My own have run dry. The men have begun to look upon us as if we are all weak yet we have survived the same trials as they. I cannot bring myself to think on the numbers we lost in battle. Yet I cannot force the images of my own losses from my mind. And now in a time when our people should be banding together it feels we are drifting apart. The Nords have truly won. Our once great pride and unity are shattered. If we lose hope now we will never survive. Today many, myself included, have tried to speak out in voices of reason. There can be no hope without talk of our future. We can make no difference if our spirits remain broken. [pagebreak] Eighteenth Marking, Tenth Kulniir We know that we can never again be the Snow Elves and live freely in this world. We will forever be in hiding in one form or another. But there is no reason we cannot live life with the sun and the wind against our skin. There are those here who are friends to us and plan to help us once the threat has ended. We know now to survive we must be born anew. Outside, we will appear as though we belong here. Inside, we will carry our truth and our scars.

[pagebreak]

费尔·阿加文的日记 译自伐莫文献 马卡斯城的卡塞莫 译 [pagebreak]

前言 这本日记中的日期是逐字翻译而成的。这种措辞与现代的时间度量不匹配，我们认为这是一种专门用于计算日期和月份的形式。在对古代伐莫奴隶居住区的发掘工作中，发现了类似深碗的黄铜器皿，其内部粗糙地刻着二十道标记。伐莫学者认为这个碗会被置于来自悬岩的滴水下，依据水面达到这些标记的时间，来表示时间大致的流逝。根据这本日记，该容器被称为“库尼尔”，是一种原始的伐莫计时装置。 [pagebreak]

第十库尼尔，第三标记 自从我们被迫东躲西藏以来，我觉得好像过了好几年。我不敢写我们呆在哪里，因为如果这本日记被发现的话，会波及到这座房子的好心人。雪王子旧识的这家人对我们很好。即便牺牲了，他那巨大的影响力还是在保佑着我们安全。由于我们的人数让我们越来越难以谨慎行进，因此我们一路上与许多同胞走散了。我们被迫分道扬镳，只敢走夜路。我没听到其他人去向的消息，而且恐怕永远也打听不到了。我们的生活永远改变了。 [pagebreak] 第十库尼尔，第七标记 我发现在夜里很难不去回忆过去的时光。当我休息的时候，我仍然能听见年轻人们在山谷里玩耍时的笑声。其他时候，我看见曾经在雪精灵的土地上如此常见的幸福时刻一闪而过。我努力不去沉湎于这些回忆太长时间。我们在这么个小地方一起呆太久了，对彼此都觉得烦了。即便是我们之中最坚强的人，也因为无事可做而踌躇，只想着失落的事物。我每天早上醒过来，都会看见那些绝望的面孔，然后想起来我们身处何处以及我们所留在身后的一切。我们都渴望有朝一日能从藏身之处走出来，再次自由自在地行走在阳光下。不过我担心我们已经完全失去了期盼这一天将会来临的希望了。 [pagebreak] 第十库尼尔，第十标记 我厌倦了女人和孩子的眼泪了。我自己的眼泪已经流尽了。男人们开始觉得我们都是弱者，然而我们同他们一样经过了重重考验。我没法想象我们在战斗中失去了多少人。我也没法忘记我自己失去的东西。而现在正是我们族人应该团结一致的时候，但是我却觉得我们渐渐疏远了起来。诺德人确实赢了。我们曾经伟大的骄傲与团结被击碎了。如果我们现在失去了希望，我们就没法活下去。如今，包括我在内的许多人，都试着发出理性之声。如果我们不谈及未来，就不会有希望。如果我们的精神依然崩溃的话，就会一事无成。 [pagebreak] 第十库尼尔，第十八标记 我们知道我们没法再作为雪精灵，在这个世界自由生活了。我们永远以这样那样的方式躲藏起来。不过我们没有理由不能在阳光和微风下生活。这里有些人是我们的朋友，他们计划在威胁结束后帮助我们。我们现在知道，为了生存我们必须重生。从外表看，我们会表现得仿佛属于这里。在心底里，我们将带着我们的真相与伤疤。

english:
[pagebreak]

MIRTIL ANGOTH JOURNAL

[pagebreak]

FOURTH EVENING STAR I USED TO DREAM OF FIGHTING IN BATTLES LIKE MY FATHER HE HAD BEGUN TEACHING ME TO FIGHT THE MOMENT I WAS ABLE TO PICK UP A BLADE MOTHER HAD ARGUED THAT I WAS TOO YOUNG BUT HE PAID HER NO MIND I CAN STILL REMEMBER THE ELATION I FELT THE FIRST TIME I BESTED FATHER IN A MATCH AND THE LOOK OF PRIDE ON HIS FACE IF IT WERE UP TO HIM I KNOW HE WOULD HAVE ALLOWED ME TO JOIN HIM IN BATTLE WITH ME AT HIS SIDE HE MAY HAVE FARED BETTER NOW WITH FATHER AND SO MANY OTHERS SLAIN THE OLD ONES CLAIM WE ARE LEFT WITH TOO FEW WARRIORS TO CONTINUE THE FIGHT I WAS NOT THE ONLY YOUNG ONE TO SPEAK OUT IN PROTEST BUT OUR SMALL VOICES WENT UNHEARD IT HAS BEEN DECIDED THAT WE MUST FLEE TO SEEK HELP AND PROTECTION EIGHTH EVENING STAR NEWS HAS REACHED US THAT THE GREAT SNOW PRINCE HAS FALLEN IN BATTLE THE URGENCY TO GO INTO HIDING HAS LEFT MANY OF US SCATTERED AND THOSE OF US STILL TOGETHER UNSURE OF WHICH DIRECTION TO TURN IN THE LONG HOURS OF NIGHT WE KEEP HUDDLED TOGETHER ALWAYS FEARING THE WORST UNTIL THE FIRST LIGHT OF THE BLESSED SUN MAY AURI EL GUIDE OUR FOOTSTEPS THIRTEENTH EVENING STAR IN THE NIGHT I OVERHEARD THE OLD ONES WHISPERING SECRETS OF THE UNDERGROUND AND THE DWEMER WHO DWELL THERE I THOUGHT BACK ON STORIES FATHER ONCE TOLD ME OF THESE DWARVES HEROIC TALES OF HONOR AND GLORY THE OLD ONES MUST KNOW OF THESE STORIES FOR IT HAS BEEN DECIDED THAT WE WILL CHANGE COURSE UPON FIRST LIGHT I FEEL HOPEFUL THAT THE DWEMER WILL HELP US TO AVENGE OUR FALLEN AND RECLAIM OUR LAND

[pagebreak]

MIRTIL ANGOTH JOURNAL

[pagebreak]

FOURTH EVENING STAR I USED TO DREAM OF FIGHTING IN BATTLES LIKE MY FATHER HE HAD BEGUN TEACHING ME TO FIGHT THE MOMENT I WAS ABLE TO PICK UP A BLADE MOTHER HAD ARGUED THAT I WAS TOO YOUNG BUT HE PAID HER NO MIND I CAN STILL REMEMBER THE ELATION I FELT THE FIRST TIME I BESTED FATHER IN A MATCH AND THE LOOK OF PRIDE ON HIS FACE IF IT WERE UP TO HIM I KNOW HE WOULD HAVE ALLOWED ME TO JOIN HIM IN BATTLE WITH ME AT HIS SIDE HE MAY HAVE FARED BETTER NOW WITH FATHER AND SO MANY OTHERS SLAIN THE OLD ONES CLAIM WE ARE LEFT WITH TOO FEW WARRIORS TO CONTINUE THE FIGHT I WAS NOT THE ONLY YOUNG ONE TO SPEAK OUT IN PROTEST BUT OUR SMALL VOICES WENT UNHEARD IT HAS BEEN DECIDED THAT WE MUST FLEE TO SEEK HELP AND PROTECTION EIGHTH EVENING STAR NEWS HAS REACHED US THAT THE GREAT SNOW PRINCE HAS FALLEN IN BATTLE THE URGENCY TO GO INTO HIDING HAS LEFT MANY OF US SCATTERED AND THOSE OF US STILL TOGETHER UNSURE OF WHICH DIRECTION TO TURN IN THE LONG HOURS OF NIGHT WE KEEP HUDDLED TOGETHER ALWAYS FEARING THE WORST UNTIL THE FIRST LIGHT OF THE BLESSED SUN MAY AURI EL GUIDE OUR FOOTSTEPS THIRTEENTH EVENING STAR IN THE NIGHT I OVERHEARD THE OLD ONES WHISPERING SECRETS OF THE UNDERGROUND AND THE DWEMER WHO DWELL THERE I THOUGHT BACK ON STORIES FATHER ONCE TOLD ME OF THESE DWARVES HEROIC TALES OF HONOR AND GLORY THE OLD ONES MUST KNOW OF THESE STORIES FOR IT HAS BEEN DECIDED THAT WE WILL CHANGE COURSE UPON FIRST LIGHT I FEEL HOPEFUL THAT THE DWEMER WILL HELP US TO AVENGE OUR FALLEN AND RECLAIM OUR LAND
englishNormalized:
[pagebreak]

mirtil angoth journal

[pagebreak]

fourth evening star i used to dream of fighting in battles like my father he had begun teaching me to fight the moment i was able to pick up a blade mother had argued that i was too young but he paid her no mind i can still remember the elation i felt the first time i bested father in a match and the look of pride on his face if it were up to him i know he would have allowed me to join him in battle with me at his side he may have fared better now with father and so many others slain the old ones claim we are left with too few warriors to continue the fight i was not the only young one to speak out in protest but our small voices went unheard it has been decided that we must flee to seek help and protection eighth evening star news has reached us that the great snow prince has fallen in battle the urgency to go into hiding has left many of us scattered and those of us still together unsure of which direction to turn in the long hours of night we keep huddled together always fearing the worst until the first light of the blessed sun may auri el guide our footsteps thirteenth evening star in the night i overheard the old ones whispering secrets of the underground and the dwemer who dwell there i thought back on stories father once told me of these dwarves heroic tales of honor and glory the old ones must know of these stories for it has been decided that we will change course upon first light i feel hopeful that the dwemer will help us to avenge our fallen and reclaim our land

fourth evening star i used to dream of fighting in battles like my father he had begun teaching me to fight the moment i was able to pick up a blade mother had argued that i was too young but he paid her no mind i can still remember the elation i felt the first time i bested father in a match and the look of pride on his face if it were up to him i know he would have allowed me to join him in battle with me at his side he may have fared better now with father and so many others slain the old ones claim we are left with too few warriors to continue the fight i was not the only young one to speak out in protest but our small voices went unheard it has been decided that we must flee to seek help and protection eighth evening star news has reached us that the great snow prince has fallen in battle the urgency to go into hiding has left many of us scattered and those of us still together unsure of which direction to turn in the long hours of night we keep huddled together always fearing the worst until the first light of the blessed sun may auri el guide our footsteps thirteenth evening star in the night i overheard the old ones whispering secrets of the underground and the dwemer who dwell there i thought back on stories father once told me of these dwarves heroic tales of honor and glory the old ones must know of these stories for it has been decided that we will change course upon first light i feel hopeful that the dwemer will help us to avenge our fallen and reclaim our land

english:
[pagebreak]

The Betrayed by Engwe Emeloth Translated from Falmer Text by Calcelmo of Markarth

[pagebreak]

nd when the Snow Prince fell to ground, The Ice Elves divided above and below. Now vanquished and brutally bound, One moment had shattered all they did know. [pagebreak] he once cool wind on their skin, Now replaced with the heat of the flame. And a pride once felt deep within, Forgotten along with their name. [pagebreak] orn from their home of ice and frost, Thrown into the pitch black dread of night. Living in fear as their minds become lost, As their eyes begin dimming the light. [pagebreak] hained and enslaved, What once was light turned to blackness. Alone and betrayed, Sinking deeper into madness.
chinese:
[pagebreak]

背离之徒 英格威·伊梅洛斯 著 译自伐莫文献 马卡斯城的卡塞莫 译

[pagebreak]

当雪王子牺牲倒地， 冰雪精灵地上地下分居两地。 如今已被征服，被残忍地束缚， 一瞬间，他们所知的一切都分崩离析。 [pagebreak] 曾经吹拂肌肤的寒风， 如今变为火焰的热量。 深藏于胸中的骄傲， 与他们的名字一同被遗忘。 [pagebreak] 冰与雪的家园被撕碎， 陷入黑夜的恐惧。 当理智开始沦丧， 当双眸的视力开始昏暗， 生活充满了恐惧。 [pagebreak] 奴役与束缚， 颠倒了曾经的白与黑。 背叛与孤独， 深陷于愈演愈烈的疯狂。

english:
[pagebreak]

Discovering Ruunvald Vol. III by Moric Sidrey

[pagebreak]

The damnable headaches! Minorne be merciful, I just can't seem to shake them. The workers have started to report them as well, but while their focus on conversation and civility may wane because of it, they have not swayed from their task. If anything, they seem to have doubled their efforts. I myself cannot seem to focus on anything other than the dig. I sit here now, studying some unearthed Nordic artifacts, yet I feel a nagging call to see how the dig is progressing. The other day without thinking, I picked up a shovel and started digging myself. Fortunately, no one seemed to find this unusual, which is a blessing. I'd hate for the Vigilants of Minorne to think that I'd lost my senses! As we dig, we uncover more Nordic ruins and architecture, but have yet to hit the main chamber. Everyday I dream we'll finally reach Ruunvald, and I can't help but reflect on what this will mean to my reputation! My family will be so proud, especially my father, Minorne. He and mother have always been interested in my studies, even if my sister Minorne was not. But I'm most excited to reveal my findings to my colleagues, Minorne and Minorne, and perhaps my mentor Minorne. Oh, won't they all be pleased?

M. Sidrey
chinese:
[pagebreak]

探索鲁恩瓦德 第三卷 墨里克·西德利 著

[pagebreak]

该死的头疼！蜜诺妮行行好，我止不住头疼。就连工人们也开始报告头疼了，尽管他们因此不那么注意交谈与礼节，但却没有偏离任务。要说有什么不同的话，他们似乎加倍努力了起来。我自己似乎没法对挖掘之外的事情集中注意力。我现在坐在这里，研究着一件出土的诺德文物，但我感到一股烦人的冲动，要去看看挖掘进展得如何。前几天，我不假思索地拿起铲子，自己开始挖掘了起来。万幸的是，没有人发现这种异常，这是好事。我不喜欢让警戒者的蜜诺妮觉得我失去了理智！ 在挖掘的过程中，我们发现了更多诺德遗迹与器物，不过还没有挖到主厅。每天我都梦见我们终于抵达了鲁恩瓦德，我忍不住想这对我的名声意味着什么！我的家族会特别骄傲，尤其是我的父亲蜜诺妮。他跟母亲总是对我的研究很感兴趣，尽管我的姐姐蜜诺妮并不是这样。不过最令人兴奋的还是向我的同事——蜜诺妮跟蜜诺妮，也许还有我的导师蜜诺妮透露我的发现。哦，他们难道不会高兴吗？

M.西德利

探索鲁恩瓦德 第三卷 墨里克·西德利 著

[pagebreak]

该死的头疼！蜜诺妮行行好，我止不住头疼。就连工人们也开始报告头疼了，尽管他们因此不那么注意交谈与礼节，但却没有偏离任务。要说有什么不同的话，他们似乎加倍努力了起来。我自己似乎没法对挖掘之外的事情集中注意力。我现在坐在这里，研究着一件出土的诺德文物，但我感到一股烦人的冲动，要去看看挖掘进展得如何。前几天，我不假思索地拿起铲子，自己开始挖掘了起来。万幸的是，没有人发现这种异常，这是好事。我不喜欢让警戒者的蜜诺妮觉得我失去了理智！ 在挖掘的过程中，我们发现了更多诺德遗迹与器物，不过还没有挖到主厅。每天我都梦见我们终于抵达了鲁恩瓦德，我忍不住想这对我的名声意味着什么！我的家族会特别骄傲，尤其是我的父亲蜜诺妮。他跟母亲总是对我的研究很感兴趣，尽管我的姐姐蜜诺妮并不是这样。不过最令人兴奋的还是向我的同事——蜜诺妮跟蜜诺妮，也许还有我的导师蜜诺妮透露我的发现。哦，他们难道不会高兴吗？

m.西德利
agentText:
Addon: Dawnguard EDID: DLC1RuunvaldJournal04 Record: BOOK:DESC SID: 001FE3 English: [pagebreak]

Discovering Ruunvald Vol. III by Moric Sidrey

[pagebreak]

The damnable headaches! Minorne be merciful, I just can't seem to shake them. The workers have started to report them as well, but while their focus on conversation and civility may wane because of it, they have not swayed from their task. If anything, they seem to have doubled their efforts. I myself cannot seem to focus on anything other than the dig. I sit here now, studying some unearthed Nordic artifacts, yet I feel a nagging call to see how the dig is progressing. The other day without thinking, I picked up a shovel and started digging myself. Fortunately, no one seemed to find this unusual, which is a blessing. I'd hate for the Vigilants of Minorne to think that I'd lost my senses! As we dig, we uncover more Nordic ruins and architecture, but have yet to hit the main chamber. Everyday I dream we'll finally reach Ruunvald, and I can't help but reflect on what this will mean to my reputation! My family will be so proud, especially my father, Minorne. He and mother have always been interested in my studies, even if my sister Minorne was not. But I'm most excited to reveal my findings to my colleagues, Minorne and Minorne, and perhaps my mentor Minorne. Oh, won't they all be pleased?

M. Sidrey Chinese: [pagebreak]

探索鲁恩瓦德 第三卷 墨里克·西德利 著

[pagebreak]

english:
[pagebreak]

Discovering Ruunvald Vol. IV by Moric Sidrey

[pagebreak]

I have found my muse and her name is Minorne. Reading back over old journals, I realize she has called to me from deep inside Ruunvald. She is the voice I've been hearing, the one who has called me ever downwards into the mountain. The Vigilants, the workers, they hear her too! What joy to learn that I am not alone in her love! Oh, Minorne, how would we have ever found this place without you! As I write this we are digging out the last bit of rock to get to you. Those without tools have started using their hands! I cannot write anymore, I must get back to work. Ruunvald awaits!

M. Sidrey
chinese:
[pagebreak]

探索鲁恩瓦德 第四卷 墨里克·西德利 著

[pagebreak]

我发现了我冲动的来源，她的名字是蜜诺妮。回顾以前的日记，我发现是她在鲁恩瓦德深处召唤我。她就是我一直听到的那个声音，那个一直呼唤我进入这座山的声音。警戒者还有工人们，他们也听到她的声音了！得知她并非独宠我一人是何等乐事啊！啊，蜜诺妮，如果没有您，我们怎会发现此地！当我写这篇日记的时候，我们正在挖掘最后一块岩石，以便找到您。那些没有工具的人已经开始上手了！我不能再写了，我必须回去工作。鲁恩瓦德正等着呢！

M.西德利

THIRD MARKING TENTH KULNIIR IT FEELS LIKE YEARS SINCE WE WERE FORCED INTO HIDING I DARE NOT WRITE WHERE WE STAY FOR FEAR OF ENDANGERING THE GOOD PEOPLE OF THIS HOUSE SHOULD THIS DIARY BE DISCOVERED WE HAVE BEEN SHOWN A KINDNESS BY THIS FAMILY ONCE KNOWN TO THE SNOW PRINCE EVEN IN DEATH HIS GREAT INFLUENCE HAS ENSURED OUR SAFETY WE WERE SEPARATED FROM MANY OF OUR KIN ALONG THE ROAD WHEN IT BECAME INCREASINGLY DIFFICULT TO TRAVEL DISCREETLY IN OUR NUMBERS WE WERE FORCED TO GO OUR SEPARATE WAYS AND TRAVEL ONLY AT NIGHT I HAVE HEARD NO NEWS OF WHERE THE OTHERS MAY HAVE GONE AND FEAR I NEVER SHALL OUR LIVES ARE FOREVER CHANGED SEVENTH MARKING TENTH KULNIIR IN THE NIGHT I FIND IT DIFFICULT NOT TO FOCUS ON TIMES PAST THERE ARE MOMENTS IN MY REST WHEN I STILL HEAR THE LAUGHTER OF YOUNG ONES AT PLAY IN THE VALLEY OTHER TIMES I SEE THE PALE FLICKER OF HAPPY MOMENTS WHICH WERE ONCE SO COMMON IN THE LAND OF THE SNOW ELVES I TRY NOT TO DWELL ON THESE MEMORIES TOO LONG OFTEN OUR SURROUNDINGS MAKE IT IMPOSSIBLE TO DWELL ON ANY HAPPINESS WE HAVE BEEN LOCKED TOGETHER IN SUCH CLOSE QUARTERS FOR SO LONG WE GROW TIRED OF EACH OTHERS COMPANY EVEN THE STRONGEST OF US HAVE FALTERED WITH NOTHING TO DO BUT THINK ON WHAT IS LOST I WAKE EACH DAY TO FORLORN FACES AND AM REMINDED OF WHERE WE ARE AND ALL WE HAVE LEFT BEHIND WE ARE ALL YEARNING FOR A DAY WHEN WE CAN EMERGE FROM HIDING AND WALK FREELY IN THE LIGHT ONCE MORE BUT I FEAR WE ARE LOSING ALL HOPE THAT SUCH A DAY WILL EVER COME TENTH MARKING TENTH KULNIIR I TIRE OF THE TEARS OF WOMEN AND CHILDREN MY OWN HAVE RUN DRY THE MEN HAVE BEGUN TO LOOK UPON US AS IF WE ARE ALL WEAK YET WE HAVE SURVIVED THE SAME TRIALS AS THEY I CANNOT BRING MYSELF TO THINK ON THE NUMBERS WE LOST IN BATTLE YET I CANNOT FORCE THE IMAGES OF MY OWN LOSSES FROM MY MIND AND NOW IN A TIME WHEN OUR PEOPLE SHOULD BE BANDING TOGETHER IT FEELS WE ARE DRIFTING APART THE NORDS HAVE TRULY WON OUR ONCE GREAT PRIDE AND UNITY ARE SHATTERED IF WE LOSE HOPE NOW WE WILL NEVER SURVIVE TODAY MANY MYSELF INCLUDED HAVE TRIED TO SPEAK OUT IN VOICES OF REASON THERE CAN BE NO HOPE WITHOUT TALK OF OUR FUTURE WE CAN MAKE NO DIFFERENCE IF OUR SPIRITS REMAIN BROKEN EIGHTEENTH MARKING TENTH KULNIIR WE KNOW THAT WE CAN NEVER AGAIN BE THE SNOW ELVES AND LIVE FREELY IN THIS WORLD WE WILL FOREVER BE IN HIDING IN ONE FORM OR ANOTHER BUT THERE IS NO REASON WE CANNOT LIVE LIFE WITH THE SUN AND THE WIND AGAINST OUR SKIN THERE ARE THOSE HERE WHO ARE FRIENDS TO US AND PLAN TO HELP US ONCE THE THREAT HAS ENDED WE KNOW NOW TO SURVIVE WE MUST BE BORN ANEW OUTSIDE WE WILL APPEAR WE BELONG HERE INSIDE WE WILL CARRY OUR TRUTH AND OUR SCARS

englishNormalized:
[pagebreak]

faire agarwen life record

[pagebreak]

third marking tenth kulniir it feels like years since we were forced into hiding i dare not write where we stay for fear of endangering the good people of this house should this diary be discovered we have been shown a kindness by this family once known to the snow prince even in death his great influence has ensured our safety we were separated from many of our kin along the road when it became increasingly difficult to travel discreetly in our numbers we were forced to go our separate ways and travel only at night i have heard no news of where the others may have gone and fear i never shall our lives are forever changed seventh marking tenth kulniir in the night i find it difficult not to focus on times past there are moments in my rest when i still hear the laughter of young ones at play in the valley other times i see the pale flicker of happy moments which were once so common in the land of the snow elves i try not to dwell on these memories too long often our surroundings make it impossible to dwell on any happiness we have been locked together in such close quarters for so long we grow tired of each others company even the strongest of us have faltered with nothing to do but think on what is lost i wake each day to forlorn faces and am reminded of where we are and all we have left behind we are all yearning for a day when we can emerge from hiding and walk freely in the light once more but i fear we are losing all hope that such a day will ever come tenth marking tenth kulniir i tire of the tears of women and children my own have run dry the men have begun to look upon us as if we are all weak yet we have survived the same trials as they i cannot bring myself to think on the numbers we lost in battle yet i cannot force the images of my own losses from my mind and now in a time when our people should be banding together it feels we are drifting apart the nords have truly won our once great pride and unity are shattered if we lose hope now we will never survive today many myself included have tried to speak out in voices of reason there can be no hope without talk of our future we can make no difference if our spirits remain broken eighteenth marking tenth kulniir we know that we can never again be the snow elves and live freely in this world we will forever be in hiding in one form or another but there is no reason we cannot live life with the sun and the wind against our skin there are those here who are friends to us and plan to help us once the threat has ended we know now to survive we must be born anew outside we will appear we belong here inside we will carry our truth and our scars

I've been given an Initiate's Ewer which will ultimately provide me access to the Chantry's Inner Sanctum where both Arch-Curate Vyrthur and Auriel's Bow are located.

english: While exploring the Soul Cairn, I've come across the soul of a strange Dunmer named Jiub. He claims to be writing the second volume of his life's story but lacks the notes from his first in order to complete it. He's asked for my help in locating the pages which are likely spread across the entire Soul Cairn... quite a daunting task.
chinese: 在探索灵魂石冢时，我遇到了一个叫做鸠步的奇怪丹莫鬼魂。他声称正在书写人生传记的第二卷，但却缺少了第一卷的笔记。他让我帮他寻找那些可能散落在灵魂石冢各处的手稿……这可是个相当艰巨的任务。

He claims to be writing the second volume of his life's story but lacks the notes from his first in order to complete it. He's asked for my help in locating the pages which are likely spread across the entire Soul Cairn... quite a daunting task.
Chinese: 在探索灵魂石冢时，我遇到了一个叫做鸠步的奇怪丹莫鬼魂。他声称正在书写人生传记的第二卷，但却缺少了第一卷的笔记。他让我帮他寻找那些可能散落在灵魂石冢各处的手稿……这可是个相当艰巨的任务。

english:
[pagebreak]

The Rise and Fall of Saint Jiub the Eradicator Hero of Morrowind and Savior of the Dunmer by Jiub

[pagebreak]

am a hunter. I am a redeemer. I am Jiub. The tale of my rise to glory begins in the ash wastes of Morrowind. I rode alone, weapon at my side and the burning wind stinging my face. My quest was arduous, but necessary to ensure the survival of the Dunmer people. A pestilence was creeping across the ashlands, a menace with an insatiable hunger that plagued innocent travelers simply trying to get home. It was my self-sworn task to hunt them down one-by-one and drive them from the skies. Their fury knew no bounds and their war cry resonated across the land. They were the notorious cliff racers, and they had to be destroyed. On a particularly hot day during Sun's Height, I was tracking what I called a Lingerer... a cliff racer without a nest. He was a particularly feisty one too, leading me on a merry chase across almost three miles of ash dunes. I had managed to take a piece out of one of his wings in an earlier scuffle so he couldn't maintain much of a climb, but he still had quite a bit of stamina left and he was trying to make me tire of the chase. Almost two solid hours passed and my silt strider was tiring, but I couldn't give up... I had sworn to eliminate the foul beasts to the last and I wasn't about to let it go. If I was going to stop the thing, I'd have to do it fast. I pulled my longbow from my back and nocked my last arrow. I took a deep breath and pulled, trying to keep the cliff racer in my sights. It was literally a longshot with the beast gaining distance and the silt strider bouncing me around at full gallop. Finally, with a silent prayer, I released the string. The arrow sang through the air like a howling demon as it sliced its way towards its target. Finally, just as it crested the lip of a foyada, the arrow struck it in the midsection. It let out a horrible cry and fell out of sight. My cries of triumph were quickly stifled by the sound of over a hundred wings. Rising from the foyada was an entire colony of cliff racers and they were out for blood. The blasted thing had led me right to their nest and sacrificed itself with the intent of feeding me to its brood. It was a trap. The damned things had become much too clever. Knowing this was likely the end, I jumped down from the silt strider and hit the back of its leg with the flat of my glass blade. There was no need for the innocent thing to die here today because of my stupidity. As the ash cloud cleared from being stirred up by its massive legs, the cliff racer brood approached. I held my sword high and prepared for the worst. The battle lasted two full days. I was beaten, clawed, bitten and knocked down more times than I care to remember. In the end, seventy-six cliff racers were slaughtered. I was knee-deep in their corpses and my body on the verge of collapse. But I had survived. I smiled to the heavens and all went black. When I awoke, all I felt was my back on a cold stone floor. Every muscle in my body was on fire, and my vision was blurred. Slowly, I tried to climb to my feet. It took several agonizing minutes, but I finally managed to do it. As my eyes adjusted to the dim light of my new surroundings, I realized that I was standing before Lord Vivec himself. He was simply staring at me... floating above his throne and staring at me with his piercing eyes. When I began to prostrate myself as a sign of respect, he held up one of his hands as if to say it wasn't necessary. Was I dead? Was Lord Vivec pleased with me? Was he about to strike me down in anger for my somewhat sordid past? Suddenly I understood everything. Suddenly I realized that I was brought here for a reason. I should have died in those ash wastes, but Lord Vivec must have seen something inside me that he hadn't seen in millennia and decided to spare me from my fate. Thus began my ascent to Sainthood. Thus began the rise of Jiub!
chinese:
[pagebreak]

晨风的除害英雄 丹莫的救世主 圣鸠步的崛起与陨落 鸠步 著

[pagebreak]

我乃猎手。我乃救世主。我即是鸠步。 我走向荣耀的传说始于晨风的灰烬荒地。我独乘一骑，身负武器，燃烧的风刺痛了脸颊。我的任务艰巨，然而必须要保障丹莫人民的生存安全。一场瘟疫正在灰地蔓延，一场无尽贪食的威胁困扰着想要归家的旅人。我暗暗发誓，要把它们一个个从天上击落。它们的怒火永无止境，它们的叫声响彻大陆。它们就是臭名昭著的峭壁行者，它们必须被摧毁殆尽。 在日高月一个特别炎热的日子里，我跟踪着一只没有巢穴的峭壁行者……它名叫逗留者。它也相当活跃，让我高兴地追着他跑过了将近三里的灰丘。在早些的追逐中，我设法把它的翅膀削掉了一块，所以它爬不了多久，不过它仍然有不少耐力，试图让我厌倦追逐。近过去了足足两个小时，我的泥沙漫步者开始累了，但是我不能放弃……我曾发誓要彻底消灭这些肮脏的畜生，我不想就此罢休。如果我想阻止它，就得快点动手。 我从背上取下弓，搭上最后一支箭矢。我深吸一口气，拉弓，努力让峭壁行者保持在视野之内。这可真是一次高难度远射，那畜生不断向远方跑去，泥沙漫步者全速飞奔把我颠来颠去。最终，随着无声的祷告，我放开弓弦。箭矢从空中呼啸而过，如同一只嚎叫的恶魔，飞向它的目标。最后，当逗留者抵达佛亚达的边缘时，箭射中了它的腹部。它发出骇人的叫声，消失在了视线之外。 我那胜利的叫喊声很快被无数翅膀拍打的声响压了过去。佛亚达中飞出了一大群峭壁行者，它们一心渴求杀戮。那个混球把我引向它们的巢穴，然后自我牺牲，把我去喂它的崽子。这是个陷阱。这些天杀的东西太狡猾了。知道自己可能大限将至，我从泥沙漫步者上跳下来，用玻璃剑的剑面打了它的腿背。没必要因为我的愚蠢而让无辜的生命在今天死在这里。当泥沙漫步者结实的腿扬起的灰云散去时，峭壁行者的崽子们接近了。我把剑高高举起，做好了最坏的打算。 战斗持续了足足两天。我已经记不清我被打、被抓、被咬、被击倒多少次了。最终，我杀死了七十六只峭壁行者。我站在齐膝深的尸骸中，身体濒临崩溃。不过我活了下来。我向天空微笑，一切就陷入了黑暗。 当我醒过来时，我感觉我躺在冰冷的石头地板上。我身上每一寸肌肉都在燃烧，我的视力也模糊了。我试着慢慢爬起来。这几分钟痛苦难熬，不过最终我做到了。当我的眼睛适应新环境的昏暗光线时，我发现我就站在维威克大君本尊面前。他只是盯着我……悬浮在他的王座上，用他那锐利的目光盯着我。当我下拜以示尊重时，他抬了抬手，似乎在说不必多礼。我死了吗？维威克大君对我满意吗？他会不会因为我略显卑鄙的过去而愤然将我毁灭呢？ 突然之间，我明白了一切。突然之间，我明白我并非无缘无故被带到此处。我本该死在那灰烬中，不过维威克大君一定看到了我内心中某些他千年未曾得见的东西，并且决定让我摆脱命运。 就这样，我的升圣之路开始了。就这样，鸠步的崛起开始了！

They were the notorious cliff racers, and they had to be destroyed. On a particularly hot day during Sun's Height, I was tracking what I called a Lingerer... a cliff racer without a nest. He was a particularly feisty one too, leading me on a merry chase across almost three miles of ash dunes. I had managed to take a piece out of one of his wings in an earlier scuffle so he couldn't maintain much of a climb, but he still had quite a bit of stamina left and he was trying to make me tire of the chase. Almost two solid hours passed and my silt strider was tiring, but I couldn't give up... I had sworn to eliminate the foul beasts to the last and I wasn't about to let it go. If I was going to stop the thing, I'd have to do it fast. I pulled my longbow from my back and nocked my last arrow. I took a deep breath and pulled, trying to keep the cliff racer in my sights. It was literally a longshot with the beast gaining distance and the silt strider bouncing me around at full gallop. Finally, with a silent prayer, I released the string. The arrow sang through the air like a howling demon as it sliced its way towards its target. Finally, just as it crested the lip of a foyada, the arrow struck it in the midsection. It let out a horrible cry and fell out of sight. My cries of triumph were quickly stifled by the sound of over a hundred wings. Rising from the foyada was an entire colony of cliff racers and they were out for blood. The blasted thing had led me right to their nest and sacrificed itself with the intent of feeding me to its brood. It was a trap. The damned things had become much too clever. Knowing this was likely the end, I jumped down from the silt strider and hit the back of its leg with the flat of my glass blade. There was no need for the innocent thing to die here today because of my stupidity. As the ash cloud cleared from being stirred up by its massive legs, the cliff racer brood approached. I held my sword high and prepared for the worst. The battle lasted two full days. I was beaten, clawed, bitten and knocked down more times than I care to remember. In the end, seventy-six cliff racers were slaughtered. I was knee-deep in their corpses and my body on the verge of collapse. But I had survived. I smiled to the heavens and all went black. When I awoke, all I felt was my back on a cold stone floor. Every muscle in my body was on fire, and my vision was blurred. Slowly, I tried to climb to my feet. It took several agonizing minutes, but I finally managed to do it. As my eyes adjusted to the dim light of my new surroundings, I realized that I was standing before Lord Vivec himself. He was simply staring at me... floating above his throne and staring at me with his piercing eyes. When I began to prostrate myself as a sign of respect, he held up one of his hands as if to say it wasn't necessary. Was I dead? Was Lord Vivec pleased with me? Was he about to strike me down in anger for my somewhat sordid past? Suddenly I understood everything. Suddenly I realized that I was brought here for a reason. I should have died in those ash wastes, but Lord Vivec must have seen something inside me that he hadn't seen in millennia and decided to spare me from my fate. Thus began my ascent to Sainthood. Thus began the rise of Jiub! Chinese: [pagebreak]

晨风的除害英雄 丹莫的救世主 圣鸠步的崛起与陨落 鸠步 著

[pagebreak]

我乃猎手。我乃救世主。我即是鸠步。 我走向荣耀的传说始于晨风的灰烬荒地。我独乘一骑，身负武器，燃烧的风刺痛了脸颊。我的任务艰巨，然而必须要保障丹莫人民的生存安全。一场瘟疫正在灰地蔓延，一场无尽贪食的威胁困扰着想要归家的旅人。我暗暗发誓，要把它们一个个从天上击落。它们的怒火永无止境，它们的叫声响彻大陆。它们就是臭名昭著的峭壁行者，它们必须被摧毁殆尽。 在日高月一个特别炎热的日子里，我跟踪着一只没有巢穴的峭壁行者……它名叫逗留者。它也相当活跃，让我高兴地追着他跑过了将近三里的灰丘。在早些的追逐中，我设法把它的翅膀削掉了一块，所以它爬不了多久，不过它仍然有不少耐力，试图让我厌倦追逐。近过去了足足两个小时，我的泥沙漫步者开始累了，但是我不能放弃……我曾发誓要彻底消灭这些肮脏的畜生，我不想就此罢休。如果我想阻止它，就得快点动手。 我从背上取下弓，搭上最后一支箭矢。我深吸一口气，拉弓，努力让峭壁行者保持在视野之内。这可真是一次高难度远射，那畜生不断向远方跑去，泥沙漫步者全速飞奔把我颠来颠去。最终，随着无声的祷告，我放开弓弦。箭矢从空中呼啸而过，如同一只嚎叫的恶魔，飞向它的目标。最后，当逗留者抵达佛亚达的边缘时，箭射中了它的腹部。它发出骇人的叫声，消失在了视线之外。 我那胜利的叫喊声很快被无数翅膀拍打的声响压了过去。佛亚达中飞出了一大群峭壁行者，它们一心渴求杀戮。那个混球把我引向它们的巢穴，然后自我牺牲，把我去喂它的崽子。这是个陷阱。这些天杀的东西太狡猾了。知道自己可能大限将至，我从泥沙漫步者上跳下来，用玻璃剑的剑面打了它的腿背。没必要因为我的愚蠢而让无辜的生命在今天死在这里。当泥沙漫步者结实的腿扬起的灰云散去时，峭壁行者的崽子们接近了。我把剑高高举起，做好了最坏的打算。 战斗持续了足足两天。我已经记不清我被打、被抓、被咬、被击倒多少次了。最终，我杀死了七十六只峭壁行者。我站在齐膝深的尸骸中，身体濒临崩溃。不过我活了下来。我向天空微笑，一切就陷入了黑暗。 当我醒过来时，我感觉我躺在冰冷的石头地板上。我身上每一寸肌肉都在燃烧，我的视力也模糊了。我试着慢慢爬起来。这几分钟痛苦难熬，不过最终我做到了。当我的眼睛适应新环境的昏暗光线时，我发现我就站在维威克大君本尊面前。他只是盯着我……悬浮在他的王座上，用他那锐利的目光盯着我。当我下拜以示尊重时，他抬了抬手，似乎在说不必多礼。我死了吗？维威克大君对我满意吗？他会不会因为我略显卑鄙的过去而愤然将我毁灭呢？ 突然之间，我明白了一切。突然之间，我明白我并非无缘无故被带到此处。我本该死在那灰烬中，不过维威克大君一定看到了我内心中某些他千年未曾得见的东西，并且决定让我摆脱命运。 就这样，我的升圣之路开始了。就这样，鸠步的崛起开始了！

english:
[pagebreak]

The Sultry Argonian Bard Volume 1 By: Ellya Erdain

[pagebreak]

Act VI, Scene II, continued Croon-Tail My lady, I could never perform your request! Ellya Erdain Oh? Is it too fast for you? Croon-Tail I fear that it might damage my instrument. Ellya Erdain Ah. But you seem to handle your instrument so well, my darling. Croon-Tail You flatter me, my lady. Ellya Erdain Yes, well it is such a large and magnificent piece. May I hold it? Croon-Tail Goodness no! The innkeeper would never approve of such a public display. Ellya Erdain Then may I suggest a private performance? Perhaps, away from the noise of the inn where we both may enjoy your tremendous talent. Croon-Tail Surely you don't mean for me to accompany you to your room? Ellya Erdain Indeed I do, my sweet. Indeed I do. END OF ACT VI, SCENE II
chinese:
[pagebreak]

性感迷人的阿尔贡诗人 卷一 艾丽娅·厄迪恩 著

[pagebreak]

第六幕，第二场，续 尾巴唱唱： 夫人啊，我永远也没法满足您的要求！ 艾丽娅·厄迪恩： 哦？对你来说太快了吗？ 尾巴唱唱： 我怕伤到我的乐器。 艾丽娅·厄迪恩： 啊。可是亲爱的，你那把乐器似乎演奏得挺不错呢。 尾巴唱唱： 夫人，您过奖了。 艾丽娅·厄迪恩： 没错，这可真是一部雄伟又华美的杰作啊。我能拿着吗？ 尾巴唱唱： 老天，不要！旅店老板可坚决不许做这种公演啊。 艾丽娅·厄迪恩： 那我能办场私人演出吗？也许，找个远离旅店喧嚣的地方，在那儿咱俩都能欣赏你那非凡的天赋了。 尾巴唱唱： 您该不是要我陪您回屋吧？ 艾丽娅·厄迪恩： 我就是，亲爱的。我就是。 第六幕，第二场结束。

english: When a vampire drains your life, you may become infected with Sanguinare Vampiris. If you do not cure this disease within three days, you will become a vampire. As a vampire, you will gain several abilities that reflect your undead and supernatural nature. You will also be afflicted with the vampire's weakness to sunlight, and suffer ill effects when traveling outdoors during the daytime. As time passes, both your powers and your weaknesses will grow in strength. In order to offset these weaknesses, you must "feed" on a humanoid subject. To feed on someone, approach them while they are asleep and activate them. Once you have fed, your powers and weaknesses will be reduced to their lowest level, and the cycle will begin again.
chinese: 当吸血鬼吸收你的生命时，你可能会感染血腥吸血热。如果不在三天内治好，就会变成吸血鬼。 作为吸血鬼，你会获得具有亡灵与超自然本质的能力。你也会因吸血鬼的弱性而饱受阳光折磨，白天在外活动将会感到不适。随着时间流逝，你的力量与弱点都会增强。 为了弥补弱点，你必须“进食”人形目标。在他们睡着时接近并吸食他们。吃饱后，你的力量和弱点将被降低回最低水平，循环将再次开始。
englishNormalized: when a vampire drains your life, you may become infected with sanguinare vampiris. if you do not cure this disease within three days, you will become a vampire. as a vampire, you will gain several abilities that reflect your undead and supernatural nature. you will also be afflicted with the vampire's weakness to sunlight, and suffer ill effects when traveling outdoors during the daytime. as time passes, both your powers and your weaknesses will grow in strength. in order to offset these weaknesses, you must "feed" on a humanoid subject. to feed on someone, approach them while they are asleep and activate them. once you have fed, your powers and weaknesses will be reduced to their lowest level, and the cycle will begin again.
chineseNormalized: 当吸血鬼吸收你的生命时，你可能会感染血腥吸血热。如果不在三天内治好，就会变成吸血鬼。 作为吸血鬼，你会获得具有亡灵与超自然本质的能力。你也会因吸血鬼的弱性而饱受阳光折磨，白天在外活动将会感到不适。随着时间流逝，你的力量与弱点都会增强。 为了弥补弱点，你必须“进食”人形目标。在他们睡着时接近并吸食他们。吃饱后，你的力量和弱点将被降低回最低水平，循环将再次开始。
agentText:
Addon: Dawnguard
EDID: HelpVampirism
Record: MESG:DESC
SID: 0026F0
English: When a vampire drains your life, you may become infected with Sanguinare Vampiris. If you do not cure this disease within three days, you will become a vampire. As a vampire, you will gain several abilities that reflect your undead and supernatural nature. You will also be afflicted with the vampire's weakness to sunlight, and suffer ill effects when traveling outdoors during the daytime. As time passes, both your powers and your weaknesses will grow in strength. In order to offset these weaknesses, you must "feed" on a humanoid subject. To feed on someone, approach them while they are asleep and activate them. Once you have fed, your powers and weaknesses will be reduced to their lowest level, and the cycle will begin again.
Chinese: 当吸血鬼吸收你的生命时，你可能会感染血腥吸血热。如果不在三天内治好，就会变成吸血鬼。 作为吸血鬼，你会获得具有亡灵与超自然本质的能力。你也会因吸血鬼的弱性而饱受阳光折磨，白天在外活动将会感到不适。随着时间流逝，你的力量与弱点都会增强。 为了弥补弱点，你必须“进食”人形目标。在他们睡着时接近并吸食他们。吃饱后，你的力量和弱点将被降低回最低水平，循环将再次开始。

english: After awakening from the Dreamstride, I was able to remove the barrier to the inner sanctum. I must now follow Erandur to the Skull of Corruption so it can be rendered inert.
chinese: 从梦中漫步醒来后，我解除了通往内部密室的屏障。我现在必须跟着依兰杜尔找到堕落头骨并摧毁它。
englishNormalized: after awakening from the dreamstride, i was able to remove the barrier to the inner sanctum. i must now follow erandur to the skull of corruption so it can be rendered inert.
chineseNormalized: 从梦中漫步醒来后，我解除了通往内部密室的屏障。我现在必须跟着依兰杜尔找到堕落头骨并摧毁它。
agentText:
Addon: Dawnguard
EDID: DA16
Record: QUST:CNAM
SID: 002A2F
English: After awakening from the Dreamstride, I was able to remove the barrier to the inner sanctum. I must now follow Erandur to the Skull of Corruption so it can be rendered inert.
Chinese: 从梦中漫步醒来后，我解除了通往内部密室的屏障。我现在必须跟着依兰杜尔找到堕落头骨并摧毁它。

english: From a tome obtained in the temple's library, Erandur has discovered a way to breach the inner sanctum. By obtaining a special liquid, we'll be able to perform a Dreamstride... travel a distance using another subject's dreams.
chinese: 从神殿图书馆的典籍中，依兰杜尔发现了攻破密室的方法。我们需要一种特殊的液体来实现梦中漫步……利用其它对象的梦境进行远距离旅行。
englishNormalized: from a tome obtained in the temple's library, erandur has discovered a way to breach the inner sanctum. by obtaining a special liquid, we'll be able to perform a dreamstride... travel a distance using another subject's dreams.
chineseNormalized: 从神殿图书馆的典籍中，依兰杜尔发现了攻破密室的方法。我们需要一种特殊的液体来实现梦中漫步……利用其它对象的梦境进行远距离旅行。
agentText:
Addon: Dawnguard
EDID: DA16
Record: QUST:CNAM
SID: 002A33
English: From a tome obtained in the temple's library, Erandur has discovered a way to breach the inner sanctum. By obtaining a special liquid, we'll be able to perform a Dreamstride... travel a distance using another subject's dreams.
Chinese: 从神殿图书馆的典籍中，依兰杜尔发现了攻破密室的方法。我们需要一种特殊的液体来实现梦中漫步……利用其它对象的梦境进行远距离旅行。

english: Erandur has revealed that Nightcaller Temple contains the legendary Skull of Corruption, an artifact of great power. In order to end the nightmares, we must breach the inner sanctum and allow Erandur to perform a ritual on the Skull which will render it inert.
chinese: 依兰杜尔透露，唤夜神殿中藏着传说中的堕落头骨，那是一件强大的神器。为了结束噩梦，我们必须进入内部密室，并让依兰杜尔对着头骨举行仪式令其失效。
englishNormalized: erandur has revealed that nightcaller temple contains the legendary skull of corruption, an artifact of great power. in order to end the nightmares, we must breach the inner sanctum and allow erandur to perform a ritual on the skull which will render it inert.

Chinese: 依兰杜尔透露，唤夜神殿中藏着传说中的堕落头骨，那是一件强大的神器。为了结束噩梦，我们必须进入内部密室，并让依兰杜尔对着头骨举行仪式令其失效。

english: Erandur has assured me he can end the nightmares, the source of which seems to be within Nightcaller Temple, a place of worship for Vaermina. I have agreed to assist him in this endeavor and we are now on our way.
chinese: 依兰杜尔向我保证自己能够结束噩梦，噩梦的源头似乎在唤夜神殿，那里是祭拜瓦尔迷纳的地方。我同意帮他，我们现在上路了。

With it, he believes he can open the lockbox he's been studying by creating a mixture of blood that will resemble dwarven. I need to collect blood from several different races if I want to help him.

english: The tables have unexpectedly turned, and Mercer Frey has been revealed as Gallus's killer. I now find myself in Karliah's care after Mercer tried to murder me, claiming I'd outlasted my usefulness by witnessing their conversation. I've also discovered that Mercer, Gallus and Karliah were members of some sort of group calling themselves "The Nightingales" - guardians in service to the goddess Nocturnal. What they're protecting or what this has to do with the Guild is still a mystery to me.
chinese: 形势突然逆转，墨瑟·弗雷才是杀害加卢斯的凶手。我发现自己正被卡莱亚照顾，因为墨瑟试图杀我，说我因为目睹了他们的对话而失去价值。我还发现墨瑟、加卢斯、卡莱亚都是某个自称“夜莺”的组织中的成员，夜莺是女神诺克图娜尔的守护者。他们在保护什么，或者说这与公会有什么关系，对我来说仍然是谜。

english: The people of the Wrothgarian and Dragontail Mountains, Orcish smiths are prized for their craftsmanship. Orc troops in Heavy Armor are among the finest in the Empire, and are fearsome when using their Berserker Rage.

english: The clanfolk of the Western Valenwood forests, also known as "Bosmer." Wood elves make good scouts and thieves, and there are no finer archers in all of Tamriel. They have natural resistances to both poisons and diseases. They can Command Animals to fight for them.
chinese: 西方威伦森林的种族，也被称为“波斯莫”。木精灵是优秀的斥候和盗贼，全泰姆瑞尔难觅可以与之比肩的弓箭手。他们对毒素和疾病都具有天然的抵抗力。可以命令动物为其作战。

english: Also known as "Dunmer" in their homeland of Morrowind, dark elves are noted for their stealth and magic skills. They are naturally resistant to fire and can call upon their Ancestor's Wrath to surround themselves in fire.
chinese: 暗精灵在自己的家乡晨风也被称为“丹莫”，以潜行和魔法技艺闻名。他们天生对火焰的抵抗性更佳，并能召唤“先祖之怒”将自己包围在火焰中。

english: I met Septimus Signus, a brilliant but mad scholar living in the northern ice fields. He's directed me to a dwarven observatory that supposedly houses an Elder Scroll. He doesn't care about the Scroll itself, but wants me to use some kind of machine there to inscribe a dwarven lexicon with the Scroll's knowledge and bring it to him.
chinese: 我遇到了一位生活在北方冰原的学者赛普蒂莫斯·希格纳斯，他才华横溢却疯疯癫癫。他指引我去一个矮人观测台寻找上古卷轴。他并不关心卷轴本身，但希望我用某种机械把卷轴上的知识刻录在一块矮人方典中带给他。
englishNormalized: i met septimus signus, a brilliant but mad scholar living in the northern ice fields. he's directed me to a dwarven observatory that supposedly houses an elder scroll. he doesn't care about the scroll itself, but wants me to use some kind of machine there to inscribe a dwarven lexicon with the scroll's knowledge and bring it to him.

english: I met a strange hermit named Septimus Signus, who needed the knowledge of an Elder Scroll and a collection of blood to open a dwarven lockbox he had studied for years. I helped him open it. It did not contain what he had expected, but instead a book of great knowledge, the Oghma Infinium.
chinese: 我遇到了一个名叫赛普蒂莫斯·希格纳斯的古怪隐士，他需要上古卷轴的知识以及采集来的血液打开研究多年的矮人密锁箱。我帮他打开了箱子。里面没有他期望的东西，而是一本充满知识的《无限智典》

english: I've learned that Septimus Signus, a preeminent scholar of the Elder Scrolls, is currently living in the far north, studying a dwarven artifact. He might know where I can find an Elder Scroll for Paarthurnax.
chinese: 据我了解，一位研究上古卷轴的杰出学者，赛普蒂莫斯·希格纳斯住在北方遥远的地方，正在研究矮人器物。他可能知道去哪里寻找帕图纳克斯需要的上古卷轴。

english: Safe from the dangers below, Karliah, Brynjolf and I finally have a moment to breathe. Mecer Frey is dead, the Skeleton Key of Nocturnal is in my possession and, most importantly, the Nightingales have survived.
chinese: 远离了地下的危险，卡莱亚、布林乔夫和我终于有了喘息的时间。墨瑟·弗雷死了，诺克图娜尔的万能钥匙被我拿到了，最重要的是夜莺都活了下来。
englishNormalized: safe from the dangers below, karliah, brynjolf and i finally have a moment to breathe. mecer frey is dead, the skeleton key of nocturnal is in my possession and, most importantly, the nightingales have survived.
chineseNormalized: 远离了地下的危险，卡莱亚、布林乔夫和我终于有了喘息的时间。墨瑟·弗雷死了，诺克图娜尔的万能钥匙被我拿到了，最重要的是夜莺都活了下来。
agentText:
Addon: Dawnguard
EDID: TG08B
Record: QUST:CNAM
SID: 010E85
English: Safe from the dangers below, Karliah, Brynjolf and I finally have a moment to breathe. Mecer Frey is dead, the Skeleton Key of Nocturnal is in my possession and, most importantly, the Nightingales have survived.
Chinese: 远离了地下的危险，卡莱亚、布林乔夫和我终于有了喘息的时间。墨瑟·弗雷死了，诺克图娜尔的万能钥匙被我拿到了，最重要的是夜莺都活了下来。

english: I've finally located Mecer Frey within the ruins of Irkngthand. He seemed to be expecting us, and I'm afraid he led us here with the express purpose of eliminating the Nightingales once and for all.
chinese: 我终于在厄金坦德遗迹里找到了墨瑟·弗雷，他似乎在等着我们。他把我们引到这里应该是为了彻底消灭夜莺。
englishNormalized: i've finally located mecer frey within the ruins of irkngthand. he seemed to be expecting us, and i'm afraid he led us here with the express purpose of eliminating the nightingales once and for all.

SID: 010E9D
English: I've finally located Mecer Frey within the ruins of Irkngthand. He seemed to be expecting us, and I'm afraid he led us here with the express purpose of eliminating the Nightingales once and for all.
Chinese: 我终于在厄金坦德遗迹里找到了墨瑟·弗雷，他似乎在等着我们。他把我们引到这里应该是为了彻底消灭夜莺。

english: Karliah and Brynjolf have joined me in my exploration of Irkngthand, a vast dwarven ruin that supposedly contains the legendary "Eyes of the Falmer." Together we hope to stop Mercer Frey from stealing the Eyes and escaping from Skyrim with the Skeleton Key of Nocturnal.
chinese: 卡莱亚和布林乔夫加入了我对巨大矮人遗迹厄金坦德的探索，传说这里存放着“伐莫之眼”。我们希望能够制止墨瑟·弗雷偷走眼睛，然后带着诺克图娜尔的万能钥匙从天际逃走。

english: I ran in to a talking dog named Barbas who had a falling out with his master Clavicus Vile. I've retrieved the Rueful axe for Clavicus. I can keep the axe if I kill Barbas or I can return the axe, reunite Barbas and Clavicus Vile and receive the Masque of Clavicus Vile.
chinese: 我遇到了一只会说话的狗，自称巴巴斯。他和主人卡拉威库斯·维尔闹翻了。我为卡拉威库斯找回了悔恨之斧。如果我选择杀了巴巴斯，我就可以保留斧头；反之，等巴巴斯与卡拉威库斯·维尔重聚后，我能拿到卡拉威库斯·维尔的面具。
